actionable_feedback: Clarify and correct semantic scoring: if programmatic semantic validation was not performed, clear semantic_score/semantic_correctness_score fields or mark them as 'not evaluated'. If a model-only semantic judgment is being reported, document the exact basis (which artifact(s), which checks) in the memo and adjust the grader limitations text accordingly.

Provide workbook-level, machine-verifiable evidence: attach a CSV export of relevant worksheets, a plain-text formula dump (cell address -> formula), or enable openpyxl parsing in the validation environment so automated checks can confirm live formulas, named ranges, and sensitivity-test implementations.

Add an explicit 'sensitivity' worksheet and an 'assumptions' worksheet in the spreadsheet (or exported CSVs) and cite those worksheet names in the memo. Include row-level remediation arithmetic in the workbook (show original value, applied percentage change, resulting value, and recomputed margin) so remediation claims are reproducible from the workbook alone.

Fix the minor memo typo noted by the grader (variable-name inconsistency in the 'Formula' line) to avoid confusion and update the memo to explicitly label any claims that are 'memo-reported' vs 'workbook-verified'.
Complete and validate the agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json normalization so the trace artifact is machine-readable and references are consistent (no TraceNormalizationError).

Add explicit traceability mapping inside the workbook (or a separate worksheet) that maps each derived cell to its source cell addresses or to the memo calculation lines (e.g., 'R1 margin = sheet1!C4 - sheet1!D4 -> sheet1!F4') to speed external verification.

Where possible, include a small machine-readable JSON array of the machine_exception_labels (and a single JSON table) both in the memo and as a separate artifact to simplify ingestion by downstream verifiers.

feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are present and the memo provides clear packet-level calculations and a PASS gate decision. However the verifier flagged internal inconsistencies and weakly supported claims: (1) the grader states spreadsheet parsing was not performed (openpyxl unavailable) yet semantic_score fields are populated - remove or document the basis for semantic scoring; (2) claims that sensitivity tests and workbook formulas were 'executed' are only supported by the memo text because the .xlsx could not be programmatically opened - provide independent workbook-level evidence (exported CSVs or formula dump) or enable parse capability; (3) trace normalization errors and/or incomplete agent_trace.json were observed - complete and validate the trace artifact. Addressing these items will make the attempt auditable and verifiable.
